title: rush - Asynchronous and Distributed Computing
text: rush network consists multiple workers communicate via shared Redis database. worker evaluates tasks pushes corresponding results back database, illustrated Figure 1. Figure 1: communication flow worker Redis database rush network. octagon represents worker rectangle represents Redis database. worker W runs instance optimizer \(\mathcal{O}\), evaluates objective function \(f\), exchanges task information via shared Redis database. Arrows indicate flow information: workers retrieve completed tasks via $fetch_finished_tasks(), propose store new tasks via $push_running_tasks(), report results via $finish_tasks().

title: rush - Asynchronous and Distributed Computing
text: begin simple random search illustrate core concepts rush. Although random search require communication workers, introduces worker loop, tasks, manager. use Branin function \(f\) optimization target: \[f(x_1,x_2)=\left(x_2-\frac{5.1}{4\pi^2}x_1^2+\frac{5}{\pi}x_1-6\right)^2 +10\left(1-\frac{1}{8\pi}\right)\cos(x_1)+10\] function optimized domain \(x_1 \[-5, 10]\) \(x_2 \[0, 15]\). commonly used benchmark function fast evaluate yet sufficiently nontrivial.  Branin function define worker_loop function, executed worker. function repeatedly samples random point, evaluates using Branin function, writes result Redis database. takes RushWorker object (rush) objective function branin arguments. loop terminates 100 tasks evaluated. worker loop relies two principal methods. $push_running_tasks() method creates new task database, marks "running", returns unique key identifying . $finish_tasks() method takes key along result writes database, marking task "finished". $n_finished_tasks field tracks number completed tasks serves termination criterion. Marking task "running" evaluation essential random search, important algorithms use states workers’ tasks inform next proposal. Tasks basic units workers exchange information. task consists four components: unique key, computational state, input (xs), output (ys). input output lists may contain arbitrary data. Tasks pass one four computational states: "running", "finished", "failed", "queued". $push_running_tasks() method creates tasks marked "running" returns keys. Upon completion, $finish_tasks() marks tasks "finished" stores associated results. Tasks encounter errors can marked "failed" using $fail_tasks() method. fourth state, "queued", supports queue mechanism described Section 4.1. Rush manager class responsible starting, monitoring, stopping workers within network. initialized using rsh() function, requires network identifier config argument. config argument specifies configuration file used connect Redis database via redux package. Workers started using $start_workers() method accepts worker loop number workers arguments. additional named arguments forwarded worker loop function. workers run mirai daemons started mirai::daemons() function. optimization completes, results can retrieved database. $fetch_finished_tasks() method returns data.table containing task key, input, result. worker_id column identifies worker evaluated task. auxiliary information can passed $push_running_tasks() $finish_tasks() via extra argument. Printing rush object displays number running workers number tasks state. Note total tasks slightly exceeds 100 workers check stopping condition independently: multiple workers evaluate condition concurrently — example, 99 tasks finished — may create new task detecting limit reached. workers can stopped database reset using $reset() method.

title: rush - Asynchronous and Distributed Computing
text: define worker_loop function, executed worker. function repeatedly samples random point, evaluates using Branin function, writes result Redis database. takes RushWorker object (rush) objective function branin arguments. loop terminates 100 tasks evaluated. worker loop relies two principal methods. $push_running_tasks() method creates new task database, marks "running", returns unique key identifying . $finish_tasks() method takes key along result writes database, marking task "finished". $n_finished_tasks field tracks number completed tasks serves termination criterion. Marking task "running" evaluation essential random search, important algorithms use states workers’ tasks inform next proposal.
code: library(rush)  wl_random_search = function(rush, branin) {   while (rush$n_finished_tasks < 100) {      xs = list(x1 = runif(1, -5, 10), x2 = runif(1, 0, 15))     key = rush$push_running_tasks(xss = list(xs))      ys = list(y = branin(xs$x1, xs$x2))     rush$finish_tasks(key, yss = list(ys))   } }

title: rush - Asynchronous and Distributed Computing
text: Tasks basic units workers exchange information. task consists four components: unique key, computational state, input (xs), output (ys). input output lists may contain arbitrary data. Tasks pass one four computational states: "running", "finished", "failed", "queued". $push_running_tasks() method creates tasks marked "running" returns keys. Upon completion, $finish_tasks() marks tasks "finished" stores associated results. Tasks encounter errors can marked "failed" using $fail_tasks() method. fourth state, "queued", supports queue mechanism described Section 4.1.

title: rush - Asynchronous and Distributed Computing
text: Rush manager class responsible starting, monitoring, stopping workers within network. initialized using rsh() function, requires network identifier config argument. config argument specifies configuration file used connect Redis database via redux package. Workers started using $start_workers() method accepts worker loop number workers arguments. additional named arguments forwarded worker loop function. workers run mirai daemons started mirai::daemons() function. optimization completes, results can retrieved database. $fetch_finished_tasks() method returns data.table containing task key, input, result. worker_id column identifies worker evaluated task. auxiliary information can passed $push_running_tasks() $finish_tasks() via extra argument. Printing rush object displays number running workers number tasks state. Note total tasks slightly exceeds 100 workers check stopping condition independently: multiple workers evaluate condition concurrently — example, 99 tasks finished — may create new task detecting limit reached. workers can stopped database reset using $reset() method.

text: Random search evaluates configurations independently requires communication workers. next demonstrate sophisticated algorithm workers share intermediate results make early stopping decisions. tune XGBoost model mtcars dataset using median stopping rule: configuration abandoned performance given training iteration falls median completed evaluations iteration. worker samples random hyperparameter configuration trains model incrementally 5 20 boosting rounds. round, worker fetches completed tasks compares RMSE median RMSE iteration. performance falls median, worker discards configuration starts new one. loop terminates 1000 evaluations recorded. prepare dataset, initialize network, start workers. training test splits passed explicitly arguments worker loop. fetch finished tasks sort objective value. stop workers reset database.
code: wl_median_stopping = function(rush, training_ids, test_ids, mtcars_data, response) {   while (rush$n_finished_tasks < 1000) {     params = list(       max_depth = sample(1:20, 1),       lambda = runif(1, 0, 1),       alpha = runif(1, 0, 1)     )      model = NULL     for (iteration in seq(5, 20)) {        key = rush$push_running_tasks(xss = list(c(params, list(nrounds = iteration))))        model = xgboost::xgboost(         data = as.matrix(mtcars_data[training_ids, ]),         label = response[training_ids],         nrounds = if (is.null(model)) 5 else 1,         params = params,         xgb_model = model,         verbose = 0       )        pred = predict(model, as.matrix(mtcars_data[test_ids, ]))       rmse = sqrt(mean((pred - response[test_ids])^2))        rush$finish_tasks(key, yss = list(list(rmse = rmse)))        tasks = rush$fetch_finished_tasks()       ref = tasks[nrounds == iteration, rmse]       if (length(ref) > 0 && rmse > median(ref)) break     }   } } data(mtcars)  training_ids = sample(seq_len(nrow(mtcars)), 20) test_ids = setdiff(seq_len(nrow(mtcars)), training_ids) mtcars_data = mtcars[, -1] response = mtcars$mpg  config = redux::redis_config()  rush = rsh(   network = "example-median-stopping",   config = config)  mirai::daemons(4)  rush$start_workers(   worker_loop = wl_median_stopping,   n_workers = 4,   training_ids = training_ids,   test_ids = test_ids,   mtcars_data = mtcars_data,   response = response) rush$fetch_finished_tasks()[order(rmse)] worker_id max_depth    lambda     alpha nrounds     rmse          keys           <char>     <int>     <num>     <num>   <int>    <num>        <char> 1: exaggerate...        14 0.7958405 0.9596463       5 2.529367 4af09fc9-9... 2: superficia...         4 0.5109341 0.5187866       5 2.529367 5bb20d32-c... 3: concretion...        19 0.2243090 0.8495938       5 2.529367 0846ae64-3... 4: thin_albat...        17 0.8005867 0.2992678       5 2.529367 a7fa4fc3-1... 5: exaggerate...        14 0.7958405 0.9596463       6 3.409952 547e6044-9... 6: exaggerate...        14 0.7958405 0.9596463       7 3.409952 b783bada-9... 7: superficia...         4 0.5109341 0.5187866       6 3.409952 d6bc8928-f... 8: concretion...        19 0.2243090 0.8495938       6 3.409952 310a7ab3-0... 9: thin_albat...        17 0.8005867 0.2992678       6 3.409952 5bd83223-7... rush$reset()

title: rush - Asynchronous and Distributed Computing
text: implement Asynchronous Decentralized Bayesian Optimization (ADBO) (Egelé et al. 2023) demonstrates use shared task information queue mechanism. ADBO runs sequential Bayesian optimization multiple workers parallel. worker maintains surrogate model independently proposes next configuration maximizing upper confidence bound acquisition function. promote varying exploration–exploitation trade-offs across workers, \(\lambda\) parameter acquisition function sampled independently worker. worker completes evaluation, shares result via database; workers incorporate information local surrogate models next iteration. typical task lifecycle rush running finished, package also supports queue mechanism cases tasks created centrally distributed workers. initialize rush network push initial Latin hypercube sampling (LHS) design queue. Structured designs LHS can outperform random designs, generating requires global view design space. queue avoids redundant evaluations: design generated main process, workers draw tasks shared queue. worker loop first drains initial design queue using $pop_task() method, retrieves next queued task, marks "running", returns . queue empty, $pop_task() returns NULL, signaling transition model-based optimization phase. $fetch_tasks_with_state() method retrieves tasks specified states database, returning data.table containing task states, keys, inputs, results. Using $fetch_tasks_with_state() rather separate calls $fetch_running_tasks() $fetch_finished_tasks() prevents tasks appearing twice state transition occurs fetch. start four workers wait optimization complete.
code: config = redux::redis_config()  rush = rsh(   network = "example-bayesian-optimization",   config = config) lhs_points = lhs::maximinLHS(n = 25, k = 2) x1_lower = -5 x1_range = 15 x2_lower = 0 x2_range = 15  xss = lapply(1:25, function(i) {   # rescale to the domain   list(x1 = lhs_points[i, 1] * x1_range + x1_lower, x2 = lhs_points[i, 2] * x2_range + x2_lower) })  rush$push_tasks(xss = xss)  rush ── <Rush> ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── • Running Workers: 0 • Queued Tasks: 25 • Running Tasks: 0 • Finished Tasks: 0 • Failed Tasks: 0 wl_bayesian_optimization = function(rush, branin) {   repeat {     task = rush$pop_task()     if (is.null(task)) break     ys = list(y = branin(task$xs$x1, task$xs$x2))     rush$finish_tasks(task$key, yss = list(ys))   }    lambda = runif(1, 0.01, 10)    while (rush$n_finished_tasks < 100) {      archive = rush$fetch_tasks_with_state(states = c("running", "finished"))     mean_y = mean(archive$y, na.rm = TRUE)     archive["running", y := mean_y, on = "state"]      surrogate = ranger::ranger(       y ~ x1 + x2,       data = archive,       num.trees = 100L,       keep.inbag = TRUE)      xdt = data.table::data.table(x1 = runif(1000, -5, 10), x2 = runif(1000, 0, 15))     p = predict(surrogate, xdt, type = "se", se.method = "jack")     cb = p$predictions - lambda * p$se     xs = as.list(xdt[which.min(cb)])      key = rush$push_running_tasks(xss = list(xs))     ys = list(y = branin(xs$x1, xs$x2))     rush$finish_tasks(key, yss = list(ys))   } } mirai::daemons(4)  rush$start_workers(   worker_loop = wl_bayesian_optimization,   n_workers = 4,   branin = branin) rush$fetch_finished_tasks()[order(y)] worker_id        x1        x2           y          keys             <char>     <num>     <num>       <num>        <char>   1: solar_iris...  3.143166  2.537446   0.4674226 a0b0e020-6...   2: belated_mu...  2.976152  2.316954   0.5372052 8cd252dc-b...   3: solar_iris... -2.793528 11.374231   0.9800730 18a5e384-9...   4: abhorrent_...  3.271216  1.283263   1.2755703 e98973f7-e...   5: belated_mu...  9.439873  3.698819   1.8656401 8dbfed2b-b...  ---  99: metamathem...  3.951793 13.806798 149.2791306 d9f3e900-a... 100: metamathem...  8.169453 14.269042 167.0271346 b4458fbe-4... 101: abhorrent_...  5.444152 13.500456 168.5982989 79b429da-2... 102: metamathem...  7.294463 13.726338 170.3988687 673b55d3-1... 103: belated_mu...  5.955488 13.555983 174.1569378 56b3fc56-f...

text: Package website: release | dev rush R package asynchronous decentralized optimization. uses database-centric architecture workers communicate shared Redis database. support high-throughput workloads, rush combines sub-millisecond per-task overhead caching strategies reduce database operations. package integrates mlr3 ecosystem serves backend asynchronous optimization algorithms bbotk mlr3tuning.

title: Rapid Asynchronous and Distributed Computing
text: Database-centric architecture decentralized optimization without central controller. Asynchronous communication workers using Redis. Sub-millisecond per-task overhead high-throughput workloads. Efficient caching mechanism minimizes database read/write operations. Task queue support centralized tasks distribution needed. Fast data transformation Redis data.table. Scales large remote worker networks via mirai package. Worker logging directly Redis database using lgr. Minimal dependencies lightweight integration.

title: Rush — Rush
text: Tasks unit workers exchange information. main components task key, computational state, input (xs), output (ys). key unique identifier task Redis database. four possible computational states "running", "finished", "failed", "queued". input xs output ys lists can contain arbitrary data. Methods create task: $push_running_tasks(xss): Create running tasks $push_finished_tasks(xss, yss): Create finished tasks. $push_failed_tasks(xss, conditions): Create failed tasks. $push_tasks(xss): Create queued tasks. methods return key created tasks. methods work multiple tasks , xss yss lists inputs outputs. tasks fetched, xss yss unpacked names inner elements become columns returned table. example, xss stored list(list(x1 = 2, x2 = 3), list(x1 = 4, x2 = 5)) yields x1 x2 columns, xs column. inner element names must therefore unique across fields. Methods change state existing task: $finish_tasks(keys, yss): Save output tasks mark finished. $fail_tasks(keys, conditions): Mark tasks failed optionally save condition objects. $pop_task(): Pop task queue mark running. methods $pop_task(), $push_running_tasks(xss), $finish_tasks(keys, yss), $fail_tasks(keys, conditions) available RushWorker. following methods used fetch tasks: $fetch_tasks(): Fetch tasks. $fetch_finished_tasks(): Fetch finished tasks. $fetch_failed_tasks(): Fetch failed tasks. $fetch_tasks_with_state(): Fetch tasks different states . $fetch_new_tasks(): Fetch new tasks optionally block new tasks available. methods return data.table() tasks. Tasks following fields: xs: input task. ys: output task. xs_extra: Metadata created creating task. ys_extra: Metadata created finishing task. condition: Condition object task failed. worker_id: id worker created task.

what: rush: Rapid Asynchronous and Distributed Computing — rush-package
title: rush: Rapid Asynchronous and Distributed Computing — rush-package
text: Package tackle large-scale problems asynchronously across distributed network. Employing database centric model, rush enables workers communicate tasks results shared 'Redis' database. Key features include low task overhead, efficient caching, robust error handling. package powers asynchronous optimization algorithms 'bbotk' 'mlr3tuning' packages.

title: Start a worker — start_worker
text: worker_id (character(1)) Identifier worker. Keys redis specific worker prefixed worker id. network_id (character(1)) Identifier rush network. Manager workers must id. Keys Redis prefixed instance id. config (list()) Configuration Redis connection. lgr_thresholds (named character() | named numeric()) Logger threshold workers e.g. c("mlr3/rush" = "debug"). lgr_buffer_size (integer(1)) default (lgr_buffer_size = 0), log messages directly saved Redis data store. lgr_buffer_size > 0, log messages buffered saved Redis data store buffer full. improves performance logging. heartbeat_period (integer(1)) Period heartbeat seconds. heartbeat updated every heartbeat_period seconds. Must least 1 second. heartbeat_expire (integer(1)) Time live heartbeat seconds. heartbeat key set expire heartbeat_expire seconds. Must least heartbeat_period, otherwise live worker reaped lost two heartbeats. Set larger longest pause worker may experience, example garbage collection swapping, live worker wrongly declared lost can leave task inconsistent state. message_log (character(1)) Path message log files e.g. /tmp/message_logs/ message log files named message_<worker_id>.log. NULL, messages, warnings errors stored. output_log (character(1)) Path output log files e.g. /tmp/output_logs/ output log files named output_<worker_id>.log. NULL, output stored.

title: rush (development version)
text: chore: minimum R version now 3.6.0, Redis (>= 7.0) declared system requirement. refactor: Remove deprecated worker types "local" "remote". refactor: $fail_tasks(), $finish_tasks(), $pop_task(), $push_running_tasks() moved Rush RushWorker task marked failed finished worker processes . fix: $fail_tasks() $finish_tasks() now move task states atomically MULTI/EXEC worker crash mid-move can longer remove task running state without recording failed finished. refactor: deprecated methods $push_failed() $push_results() removed. Use $fail_tasks() $finish_tasks() instead. fix: $empty_queue() longer creates orphaned hash Redis database queue already empty. BREAKING CHANGE: $empty_queue() method now empties entire queue instead specified set tasks. keys conditions arguments removed. fix: $detect_lost_workers() longer marks task finished failed, loses task. fix: $detect_lost_workers() now returns worker ids actually detected lost call, instead also including workers terminated cleanly method running. fix: $pop_task() longer loses task worker crashes popping task queue marking running. task now moved atomically per-worker pending list recovered failed task $detect_lost_workers(). fix: $push_tasks(), $push_finished_tasks(), $push_failed_tasks(), $push_running_tasks() now return early called empty list tasks. fix: $fetch_tasks(), $fetch_finished_tasks(), $fetch_new_tasks() longer fail task hashes removed database. Affected tasks dropped warning. fix: $fetch_failed_tasks() related methods now return documented condition column holding whole condition object. feat: extra argument $push_tasks(), $push_running_tasks(), $finish_tasks() methods deprecated favor consistently named xss_extra yss_extra arguments. fix: $start_local_workers() longer generates unparseable worker startup code Windows temporary directory path contains quotes. temporary arguments file now deleted worker reads . fix: $stop_workers() longer errors requested worker id running. ids now skipped warning. perf: $wait_for_tasks() longer reads full finished list failed task set every poll. fix: $worker_script() longer logs Redis password. logged script shows <redacted> instead. method now returns script visibly. fix: RushWorker$new() now errors heartbeat process fails set heartbeat key within startup timeout instead silently registering dead heartbeat. heartbeat_period heartbeat_expire must now least 1 second, heartbeat_expire must least heartbeat_period seconds. fix: $reset() now also resets internal log counter, $print_log() longer skips messages network id reused reset. fix: $reset() now deletes keys single MULTI/EXEC transaction concurrent reader can longer observe half-reset network. feat: rush_plan() gains start_worker_timeout argument, sets default timeout used $wait_for_workers(). explicit timeout passed $wait_for_workers() longer overridden configuration. fix: start_worker() longer errors exit message_log output_log set. sinks now reverted log connections closed. perf: $worker_info reads workers one pipelined round trip. refactor: Large objects now stored UUID keys.

title: rush 1.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-04-24 fix: $detect_lost_workers() longer creates phantom failed tasks worker crashes task evaluations (#89). fix: $fetch_new_tasks() now correctly tracks seen results using actual cache size instead Redis counter, fixing cases new results missed duplicated. fix: $write_hashes() now requires value lists length length 1 instead recycling. Length mismatches raise error (#87). fix: rsh() longer accepts ... (ignored).

title: rush 1.0.1
text: CRAN release: 2026-04-04 fix: $start_workers() now strips enclosing environment worker_loop serialization, avoiding bloated Redis payloads function closure. fix: $fetch_tasks() related methods longer fail task parameters contain vector values instead scalars.

title: rush 1.0.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-18 feat: Add $push_finished_tasks() method. feat: Combine $wait_for_new_tasks() $fetch_new_tasks() $fetch_new_tasks() timeout argument. refactor: $wait_for_finished_tasks() method removed. perf: Cache now data.table() instead list. data_format argument removed. refactor: Seed mechanism removed. Includes with_rng_state(), is_lecyer_cmrg_seed(), get_random_seed(), set_random_seed(), make_rng_seeds() helper functions. refactor: PID longer stored tasks $push_tasks() $push_running_tasks() methods. refactor: worker_extra field renamed worker_id. refactor: $push_results() renamed $finish_tasks(). fix: Heartbeat process moves worker terminated. refactor: $push_results() deprecated. Use $finish_tasks() instead. refactor: $push_failed() deprecated. Use $fail_tasks() instead. refactor: $terminated_on_idle removed. refactor: Removed $push_priority_tasks(), $n_queued_priority_tasks, $priority_info, $fetch_priority_tasks(). fix: Redis password supported now. refactor: Remove $restart_workers() method. refactor: Squashed "terminated", "killed" "lost" worker states "terminated". feat: Added push_failed_tasks() creating failed tasks. refactor: Remove reset_cache arguments favor $reset_cache() method. refactor: network_id, config connector now active bindings validation. refactor: Removed $snapshot_schedule() $redis_info() methods. refactor: Removed $all_workers_terminated all_workers_lost active bindings. refactor: Moved $worker_states $worker_info. feat: Add "rush.max_object_size" option limit size objects stored Redis. refactor: Renamed $start_remote_workers() $start_workers(). feat: Add $reset_data() method reset task data without resetting network. fix: rush_plan() now allows script workers. fix: Default heartbeat expire now correctly calculated three times heartbeat period. fix: Message output log file connections now properly closed worker exits. perf: Network-specific Redis keys now deleted $reset() instead per worker.
